title: Comply Test Controller Response
description: Response from the comply_test_controller tool. Shape varies by scenario type: list_scenarios returns available scenarios, force_* returns state transition results, simulate_* returns simulation results.

oneOf.title: ListScenariosSuccess
oneOf.description: Lists which scenarios this seller's test controller supports

scenarios.description: Scenarios this seller has implemented. Runners and sellers MUST accept unknown scenario strings (open-for-extension) — new scenarios may be added in additive minor bumps. Adopters who advertise `query_upstream_traffic` opt in to the upstream-traffic conformance contract; storyboards that declare `check: upstream_traffic` grade `not_applicable` against adopters who do not advertise it.

oneOf.title: StateTransitionSuccess
oneOf.description: A force_* scenario successfully transitioned the entity to the target state

oneOf.title: SimulationSuccess
oneOf.description: A simulate_delivery or simulate_budget_spend scenario succeeded. For delivery: simulated contains impressions/clicks/reported_spend/conversions and cumulative contains running totals. For budget: simulated contains spend_percentage/computed_spend/budget.

simulated.description: Values injected or applied by this call. Shape depends on scenario.

cumulative.description: Running totals across all simulation calls (simulate_delivery only)

oneOf.title: ForcedDirectiveSuccess
oneOf.description: A force_create_media_buy_arm directive was registered. The directive shapes the next create_media_buy call from this caller's authenticated sandbox account into the requested arm, then is consumed. No entity transitioned — there is no media buy yet — so this branch carries 'forced' rather than previous_state/current_state.

forced.description: Echo of the registered directive. The next create_media_buy call from this sandbox account will return the named arm.

arm.description: Arm the seller will emit on the next create_media_buy response.

task_id.description: Echo of the registered task_id. Present only when arm is 'submitted' (the arm that emits a task envelope).

oneOf.title: SeedSuccess
oneOf.description: A seed_* scenario successfully pre-populated a fixture in the seller's test state

oneOf.title: UpstreamTrafficSuccess
oneOf.description: A query_upstream_traffic scenario returned the outbound HTTP calls the agent has made since the requested time window. Used by storyboard runners to apply `check: upstream_traffic` assertions. The mechanism raises the bar against unintentional façades — adapters (often LLM-generated) that satisfy AdCP schema requirements with synthetic placeholders without forwarding data upstream. Adopters self-report their own traffic; this contract is NOT an adversarial integrity check, and consumers MUST NOT treat upstream_traffic passing as cryptographic proof of adapter behavior. Adopters MUST NOT enable query_upstream_traffic against a sandbox that processes production identifier values; storyboards SHOULD use synthetic hashed inputs (e.g., the `acme-user-NNNN` form in the example below). Caller scoping: adopters MUST scope recorded_calls to traffic caused by the requesting principal's session/auth context — cross-caller traffic MUST NOT be returned regardless of `since_timestamp`. Multi-tenant sandboxes MUST key the recording buffer on the comply_test_controller invocation's auth principal, not just process-global. Secret redaction: adopters MUST recursively redact values at keys matching the case-insensitive pattern `^(authorization|credentials?|token|api[_-]?key|password|secret|client[_-]secret|refresh[_-]token|access[_-]token|bearer|session[_-]token|offering[_-]token|cookie|set[_-]cookie)$` from the recorded `payload` before emission. The redaction obligation is normative on the controller — runner-side redaction is a backstop, not a substitute.

recorded_calls.description: Outbound HTTP calls caused by the requesting principal in the requested window, ordered by `timestamp` ascending. Cross-caller calls MUST NOT appear here.

method.description: HTTP method of the outbound call.

endpoint.description: Composed `<METHOD> <URL>` string used for `endpoint_pattern` matching, e.g. 'POST https://api.tiktok.com/v2/audience/upload'. Convenience field — the runner can also reconstruct from `method` + URL components.

url.description: Full URL of the outbound call (scheme + host + path + query). Treated as untrusted agent-controlled input by report renderers — see runner-output-contract.yaml > security.rendered_output_fencing.

host.description: Host portion of the URL, useful for grouping calls by upstream platform.

path.description: Path portion of the URL (without query string).

content_type.description: Media type of the recorded `payload`, mirroring the agent's outbound `Content-Type` header (e.g., `application/json`, `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`, `multipart/form-data`, `text/plain`). Storyboard `payload_must_contain` assertions using JSONPath are valid only when content_type is `application/json` or has a `+json` suffix — runners MUST grade non-JSON payloads as `not_applicable` for path-based assertions and fall back to substring matching for `match: present` against the raw string. Required so the runner can choose the right matcher deterministically.

purpose.description: Optional adopter-supplied semantic tag for the call's role: `platform_primary` for the primary upstream platform the adapter is integrating with (e.g., a TikTok audience-upload call from a sales-social adapter); `measurement` for ancillary calls to measurement vendors (DV, IAS, Nielsen, MOAT); `identity` for ID-graph / hashing-service calls (LiveRamp, ID5, UID2); `other` for everything else (config fetches, internal telemetry). Lets storyboards scope `upstream_traffic` assertions via `purpose_filter` so a buyer-agent adapter that legitimately calls measurement vendors during a single buy step doesn't muddy the platform-primary assertion. Adopters who don't classify can omit the field; storyboards that don't filter on purpose ignore it. Self-reported, not adversarially trustworthy — same trust model as the rest of recorded_calls.

payload.description: Request body the agent sent. Object when content_type is JSON-shaped and the controller decoded it; string otherwise. Adopters MUST apply the recursive secret-key redaction described in this branch's top-level description before emission — secrets at any depth (Authorization values inlined into JSON bodies, embedded JWTs, presigned-URL tokens, OAuth refresh tokens) MUST be replaced with the literal string `[redacted]`. Storyboards that assert `payload_must_contain` or `identifier_paths` are matching against THIS field — secrets MUST be redacted but storyboard-supplied identifiers (hashed PII, request correlation values) MUST NOT be redacted. Adopters SHOULD cap individual payload size at 64 KiB; payloads exceeding that SHOULD be truncated with a trailing `[…truncated]` marker — large payloads bloat compliance reports and the LLM-rendered context windows that consume them.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the adopter recorded the outbound call. MUST reflect the adopter's wall clock at the moment the outbound request was sent (not log-flush time), and MUST be monotonically non-decreasing across recorded_calls of a single response. Used by runners to scope assertions to a specific storyboard step's window — see runner-output-contract.yaml > validation_result for the timestamp boundary semantics.

status_code.description: HTTP status code returned by the upstream. Optional — adopters MAY omit when the call was instrumented before the response arrived.

total_count.description: Total calls in the requested window before any pagination — `recorded_calls.length` may be smaller when `params.limit` truncated the response.

truncated.description: True when `total_count > recorded_calls.length`. Runners MAY raise the `limit` and re-query, but storyboards SHOULD declare assertions that fit within the default 100-call window — a truncated response is a signal that the storyboard step is causing more upstream activity than expected.

since_timestamp.description: Echo of the `since_timestamp` the runner requested (or the session-start timestamp the adopter substituted when the runner omitted it). Informational — the runner SHOULD use its own clock-bracket of when it issued the AdCP step request, not this echo, when attributing recorded_calls to a specific step. The controller is part of the adopter's claimed conformance; the echo is not adversarially trustworthy.

oneOf.title: ControllerError
oneOf.description: The scenario failed — invalid transition, unknown entity, unsupported scenario, or invalid params

current_state.description: Current state of the entity, or null if not found

data.message: Creative cr-123 transitioned from processing to approved
Delivery simulated for mb-789: 10000 impressions, 150 clicks, $150.00 spend
Budget for mb-789 set to 95% consumed ($950.00 of $1000.00)
Next create_media_buy call from this sandbox account will return the submitted arm with task_id task_async_signed_io_q2

data.error_detail: Cannot transition from archived to processing — archived is terminal

examples.description: Upstream traffic recorded after a sync_audiences storyboard step — adapter forwarded synthetic hashed members (acme-user-NNNN test vectors) to a hypothetical audience-upload endpoint. Authorization headers are NOT included; secret-bearing payload keys would be redacted to '[redacted]' before emission.
